name: Spotinfo MCP Server
what_it_does: Spotinfo MCP server enabling AI agents to query AWS EC2 Spot Instance pricing, interruption rates, and availability — comparing spot prices across instance types and availability zones, checking historical interruption frequency, identifying cost-optimal instance types for workloads, and integrating cloud cost optimization into agent-driven infrastructure planning and Kubernetes cluster optimization workflows.
use_cases: Comparing AWS Spot Instance prices across instance types from cost optimization agents
Checking spot interruption rates for reliability planning from infrastructure agents
Identifying cheapest spot instances for batch workloads from cost management agents
Optimizing Kubernetes node pools with spot instance data from platform engineering agents
Planning EMR, ECS, or Batch workloads with spot availability from data pipeline agents
Monitoring spot price changes for budget alert agents
not_for: Non-AWS clouds (Azure spot, GCP preemptible instances require different tools)
On-demand instance pricing (Spotinfo focuses on spot/preemptible pricing)
Teams not using EC2 or AWS compute services
best_when: A platform engineer or DevOps team using AWS needs AI-assisted spot instance selection — optimizing for cost versus interruption risk for batch, ML training, or Kubernetes workloads.
avoid_when: You're on Azure, GCP, or don't use AWS EC2 spot instances.

auth.notes: No authentication required for public spot pricing data. Spotinfo uses publicly available AWS spot pricing API and spot interruption data. No AWS credentials needed for basic functionality.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Completely free — uses public AWS spot pricing data
pricing.notes: Free open source tool. AWS spot pricing data is publicly available. No AWS account or credentials required.

performance.rate_limits: AWS public pricing API — generous limits for read access

known_agent_gotchas: Spot pricing data is near-real-time — prices can change rapidly in hot AZs
Interruption rate data is historical — past rates don't guarantee future interruptions
Not a real-time AWS API client — may have data freshness lag
Useful for planning, not for millisecond-precise pricing decisions
Community MCP from alexei-led — experienced cloud tools author
Covers AWS primarily — other cloud providers require different tools
agent_readiness.security_notes: No auth needed. HTTPS to AWS public endpoints. No credentials required. No user data. Community MCP. Read-only public data.

security_score_components.security_notes: No credentials. HTTPS. Public data only. No user data collected. Community MCP.
